name: Graph & Specs
summary: Specification synchronization engine and visualization system. Coordinates CLI-based sync processes, persists spec data to the file system, renders interactive graphs with ReactFlow, and provides alternative list/document/architect views.
goals: Synchronize project code into structured spec trees via external CLI processes
Persist spec data (manifest, specs, architect) to the file system with history archiving
Visualize specs as interactive node graphs with automatic Dagre layout
Provide multiple views: graph, list, document, and architect

design.overview: SyncEngine spawns CLI processes and streams progress via SSE. GraphStorage handles file I/O for manifest, specs, architect, and history archives. Frontend graphStore consumes sync SSE events and provides data to ReactFlow-based visualization components.
design.flow: User triggers sync → SyncEngine spawns CLI → Progress streamed via SSE → GraphStorage writes results → graphStore loads data → FlowGraph renders nodes/edges

acceptanceCriteria.then: the sync process runs and produces a structured spec tree

acceptanceCriteria.when: user navigates to the graph page
acceptanceCriteria.then: an interactive graph visualization is rendered

end-to-end.given: A project with source code exists
end-to-end.then: spec data is generated and visualizable in the graph view

syncMeta.baseCommitMessage: feat(sync-specs): add auto-detection status rules in Step 5 user review
